title: Fermented Canadian Digestive Biscuit Grill
description: A unique twist on traditional digestive biscuits, featuring a fermented twist and a touch of Japanese bonito flakes. Perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Preheat your grill to medium heat.
In a small bowl, rehydrate the active dry yeast in 1/2 cup warm water for 5 minutes.
Add the diluted honey to the yeast mixture and stir to combine.
Place a digestive biscuit on the grill and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
Brush the biscuit with softened butter and sprinkle with bonito flakes, sea salt, and a pinch of yeast mixture.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until the biscuit is crispy and fragrant.
For a fermented twist, allow the biscuits to cool and then store them in a sealed container in the refrigerator overnight, where they will ferment and develop a tangy flavor.
Before serving, brush with additional butter and sprinkle with bonito flakes and sea salt.
